facts.text: Chicago was founded in 1833, when fewer than 200 people settled on the American frontier.
Jean Baptiste Point du Sable, a free black trader from Haiti, became Chicago’s first permanent non‑indigenous resident in the 1770s.
O’Hare International Airport, located on Chicago’s far northwest side, was the world’s busiest airport by aircraft movements in 2005.
The Willis Tower, formerly Sears Tower, stands as Chicago’s tallest building and a landmark of its skyline.
Chicago Symphony Orchestra, founded in 1891, is one of the world’s leading orchestras and a cultural pillar of the city.
House music, a genre that blends electronic beats with soulful vocals, originated in Chicago’s underground club scene in the 1980s.
